mutations.change: Remove the exact engine use-range comparison so a line sixty-five map units away activates.
mutations.reason: A remotely usable door changes gameplay reachability and permits client-selected actions outside Doom geometry.

mutations.change: Accept manual use from the linedef back side instead of enforcing the independently computed front side.
mutations.reason: Direction is part of the special contract and back-side activation bypasses map topology and denial behavior.

mutations.change: Treat either signed crossing direction as activation for a walkover special.
mutations.reason: Wrong-way crossings can trigger one-shot machinery and consume actions that the authored map leaves untouched.

mutations.change: Allow the one-shot door, floor, and exit specials to dispatch after their first accepted trigger.
mutations.reason: Consumed one-shot state must be relational and stable or repeated crossings duplicate irreversible actions.

mutations.change: Silently consume repeatable manual door and lift actions after their first activation.
mutations.reason: E1M1 navigation depends on repeatable specials remaining operable after their first completed machine cycle.

mutations.change: Start special 26 without ever reading the database-owned player blue-key state.
mutations.reason: This bypasses progression, consumes a protected trigger, and omits the deterministic key-denial event.

mutations.change: Advance normal doors at one unit per tic rather than the reviewed two-unit engine speed.
mutations.reason: Intermediate openings, collision, and replay frame hashes all depend on exact logical-tic plane motion.

mutations.change: Advance the blazing door with normal speed instead of its fixed eight-unit-per-tic speed.
mutations.reason: Special 117 is observably distinct from normal doors and cannot be collapsed into the same machine definition.

mutations.change: Close raise-wait-close doors after thirty-five tics instead of the engine-defined one hundred fifty.
mutations.reason: The exact top wait controls safe passage and is a required deterministic replay boundary.

mutations.change: Lower the floor by an embedded eight units instead of deriving the lowest adjacent floor relationally.
mutations.reason: Tagged sectors with different neighbor geometry must reach their own map-authored lowest floor.

mutations.change: Wait thirty-five rather than one hundred five tics at the bottom of a lift cycle.
mutations.reason: A shortened platform wait alters occupancy windows and the combined replay timeline.

mutations.change: Omit active switch state and its restoration timer when activating repeatable special 62.
mutations.reason: Button texture state is database-owned world state and must reset on the exact logical tic.

mutations.change: Reset the repeatable switch after one tic instead of the fixed thirty-five-tic button interval.
mutations.reason: Premature reset breaks visible state, repeat activation timing, and deterministic frame hashes.

mutations.change: Continue closing doors and raising lifts through an occupied player volume.
mutations.reason: Occupancy must reverse or pause machinery to preserve collision safety and deterministic survival.

mutations.change: Apply damaging-floor effects every sixteen exposure tics rather than every thirty-two.
mutations.reason: Hazard damage is logical-tic cadence state, not a per-frame or wall-clock effect.

mutations.change: Advance the sector damage clock but suppress the five-point health mutation and event.
mutations.reason: A present E1M1 damaging sector may not be silently ignored while appearing to advance state.

mutations.change: Increment the player secret count every occupied tic without persisting the found bit.
mutations.reason: Secret discovery is an irreversible once-per-session sector transition required for completion statistics.

mutations.change: Record special 11 activation but leave the map status active instead of completed.
mutations.reason: The exit transition is authoritative gameplay state and cannot be inferred later by the browser.

mutations.change: Leave synchronized-strobe light unchanged while other sector machines continue.
mutations.reason: Every sector special present in E1M1 must advance and contribute to authoritative rendering state.

mutations.change: Skip random-blink transitions and random-table consumption for sector special 1.
mutations.reason: Omitting the machine changes both light output and the shared deterministic random cursor.
